findings.title: upsert_vocabulary_mastery omits required NOT NULL fields on INSERT
findings.why_it_matters: The INSERT in upsert_vocabulary_mastery only provides (id, user_id, mastery_level, updated_at). vocabulary_items has NOT NULL columns book_id (TEXT NOT NULL) and word (TEXT NOT NULL) with no DEFAULT. On a fresh insert (first time a vocab item is seen), the INSERT will fail with SQLSTATE 23502 (not_null_violation) because book_id and word have no default and are not supplied. This means the function cannot create new vocabulary items — it can only update existing ones, silently failing to upsert new items.
findings.suggested_fix: Add p_book_id TEXT, p_word TEXT, p_context TEXT DEFAULT NULL parameters to the function signature and include them in the INSERT VALUES clause. On conflict, only update mastery_level and updated_at (book_id and word are immutable once set). Alternatively, document that callers are responsible for pre-creating the row, but this breaks upsert semantics.
evidence: Line 283: `book_id TEXT NOT NULL` and `word TEXT NOT NULL` — no DEFAULT on either column
Line 498: `INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_items (id, user_id, mastery_level, updated_at) VALUES (...)` — omits book_id and word
SQLSTATE 23502 would be raised: 'null value in column book_id of relation vocabulary_items violates not-null constraint'

findings.title: search_similar_notes double-evaluates vector distance expression
findings.why_it_matters: The ORDER BY clause repeats `e.vector <=> p_query_vector` which is the same expression already computed in SELECT. pgvector does not guarantee expression deduplication across SELECT and ORDER BY in plpgsql RETURN QUERY — depending on the execution plan, this may compute the distance twice per row (no functional error, but unnecessary CPU cost at scale). Minor perf concern only.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap as a subquery or CTE: `SELECT note_id, distance FROM (SELECT e.note_id, (e.vector <=> p_query_vector)::FLOAT AS distance FROM ...) sub ORDER BY distance ASC LIMIT p_limit`. This makes the ORDER BY reference the computed alias, not recompute the expression.
evidence: Line 542: `(e.vector <=> p_query_vector)::FLOAT AS distance` in SELECT
Line 549: `ORDER BY e.vector <=> p_query_vector ASC` — same expression, not the alias `distance`

findings.title: flashcards.note_id has no FK constraint to notes table
findings.why_it_matters: flashcards.note_id is nullable UUID but lacks a REFERENCES public.notes(id) constraint. When a note is deleted, associated flashcards retain stale note_id values. This breaks any client logic that joins flashcards.note_id to notes — orphaned flashcard references are invisible at DB level. Contrasts with embeddings.note_id which has the correct ON DELETE CASCADE FK.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `note_id UUID REFERENCES public.notes(id) ON DELETE SET NULL` to flashcards. Since note_id is nullable, SET NULL on delete preserves the flashcard while clearing the stale reference. If cascade delete is desired instead, use ON DELETE CASCADE. Decision depends on product intent — advisory until intentionality is confirmed.
evidence: Compare line 205: `note_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.notes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE` in embeddings — FK is used there

residual_risks: flashcard_reviews.rating has no CHECK constraint (e.g., CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 4)) — invalid FSRS ratings could corrupt FSRS scheduling
book_highlights.review_rating is unconstrained TEXT — plan noted 'keep/dismiss' as valid values but no CHECK was added
testing_gaps: No test for upsert_vocabulary_mastery with new item (INSERT path) — would catch the missing NOT NULL fields
No test for search_similar_notes with p_limit=0 (edge: LIMIT 0 is valid SQL but returns empty; ensure client handles it)
